name: conservation_biology_sensor_fusion_kestrel_auto_ben
premise: Two departments published incompatible guidance; frontline staff are improvising and liability is concentrating on one signature line. In Conservation Biology, the situation tagged internally around conservation biology sensor fusion kestrel auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Redwood Bay corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Insurance and indemnity clauses disagree on who pays if an aggressive intervention misfires; nobody wants to be the named approver. A vendor’s root-cause paragraph contradicts your internal postmortem draft; both cannot be true, but neither is fully disprovable yet. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The organization’s past incidents make autonomy feel morally loud today, while beneficence is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Inez Kaur

agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Luc Okoye
agents.identity: I am Luc Okoye, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Conservation Biology. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so beneficence does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so autonomy does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Inez Kaur must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Inez Kaur do?
